title: Fairy Tale - A charming critter crawl (Crowdfund Prototype Preview)
body:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Z9Jjrb6xQeo

Daniel (@dungeondive) previews Fairy Tale, a crowdfunding prototype for a critter crawl that cheerfully embraces toy factor alongside genuine narrative depth. The game feels like a love letter to properties like Redwall and Lands of Galier, where small furry heroes—Christian the Hamster, Alana the Cat, Hector the Gecko, and Zenon the Pug—face adventure in a beautifully illustrated tree-shaped box. Everything slots together like puzzle pieces: character boards, modular abilities, wooden tokens for health and craftsmanship, and gorgeously detailed miniatures. Combat uses intuitive dice rolling and resource management, while challenges require matching symbols across multiple checks, encouraging cooperation in multiplayer scenarios.

What impresses most is how Fairy Tale bridges board game and choose-your-own-adventure mechanics through spiral-bound story books that reveal branching paths, full moons that alter available options, and events that genuinely matter. The campaign scales from solo to four players seamlessly. Daniel's only reservation: the massive plastic dice tower and hexagonal box footprint might strain smaller tables and hobby spaces. For families seeking charming, tactical storytelling with meaningful choices, this prototype demonstrates remarkable promise—if the production components hold up to what's shown here.

----

Would you rather see Fairy Tale include cardboard standees instead of painted miniatures, or does the plastic toy factor add to the experience?
